title: ManaBox Alternative
description: A clearer ManaBox alternative for MTG collections.

hero.title: ManaBox Alternative
hero.description: Spellvault is a ManaBox alternative for scanning cards, tracking prices, and managing inventory.

sections.title: Why choose this ManaBox alternative
sections.description: Spellvault focuses on clean scanning, collection structure, and usable price context.

items.title: Card scanning
items.description: Capture cards quickly and move them into a cleaner inventory flow.

items.title: Collection clarity
items.description: Keep binders, decks, folders, and copies easier to understand.

items.title: Price context
items.description: See value together with the rest of your collection data.

sections.title: Why switch from ManaBox
sections.description: Spellvault fits people who want collection workflows that stay clear as inventory, decks, and value tracking grow.

items.title: Keep scanning connected to inventory
items.description: Add cards quickly and move straight into organization instead of splitting the workflow.

items.title: Use clearer collection structure
items.description: Track folders, binders, decks, and copies in a way that stays understandable over time.

items.title: See value with more context
items.description: Review price movement and collection value alongside the cards and copies behind it.

sections.title: Spellvault vs ManaBox
sections.description: A quick view of the workflow differences that matter most.

sections.title: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.description: Common questions when comparing Spellvault and ManaBox.

items.title: Is Spellvault a good ManaBox alternative?
items.description: Yes. Spellvault is designed for people who want scanning, inventory, and price tracking in one cleaner workflow.

items.title: What makes Spellvault different from ManaBox?
items.description: The main difference is the focus on clearer inventory structure and collection-aware price context.

items.title: Can I use Spellvault for collection tracking and prices?
items.description: Yes. That combination is one of the main reasons to choose it as an alternative.

items.title: Who should consider a ManaBox alternative?
items.description: Collectors, stores, and players who want a more focused collection workflow should consider it.
